finding: Three sessions (March 22-30) were spent building and running the Artcraft AI image/video generation app. This project does not appear in any stated goal or north star connection field. The investment was small (0.9 MB of logs, brief build/run cycles) and has not recurred since March 30, so this may be a one-off exploration rather than a drift pattern. If Artcraft is an ongoing interest, adding it to goals would clarify the connection.

One session (March 22) built a collapse/community-resilience survival corpus -- downloading offline medical, sanitation, and food production guides. This could loosely connect to the 'd/acc' goal under Impact & Intellect, but the connection is not stated. Single session, no recurrence. If this is part of the d/acc thread, noting it in the goals connection field would prevent future false flags.

Across the 30-day window, 82 out of 182 sessions (45%) and 20.4 MB of log volume were spent in the ~/.claude config context -- building hooks, skills, security hardening, the recursive self-improvement loop, statusline, and other Claude Code meta-tooling. This clearly connects to the 'Claude Code usage as automatic/frictionless as possible' goal, but the sheer volume raises a question: is the meta-tooling investment producing proportional returns in the other goal areas (apps, writing, relationships, wellbeing)? The jhana app (22 sessions) and Intender (8+13 sessions) are progressing, but the ratio suggests the tooling may be consuming time that could go to direct output. This is an observation, not a directive -- the user may be in an intentional infrastructure-building phase.
existing_mitigation: None found. The recursive self-improvement loop itself is designed to surface this kind of pattern.
